focus: afternoon slate post the day AFTER a massive +17u day; playbook-aligned (mechanic-led, directional proof, THE BLEND voice, force an action)
mechanic: Discipline / process narrative (rotated off yesterday's call-your-shot) + forcing binary close
internalTape: Afternoon plays (directional in tweet): White Sox ML +123 (100% sharp, 2 proven, plus money, ~12:36 ET), Rangers ML (4 proven, 3 HC, 72%, ~1:11 ET), Under 11.5 Padres/Cubs (100% sharp, ~2:21 ET). Plus-money dog = White Sox +123.

hero_single.text:
+17 units yesterday.

The hardest part of a day like that isn't winning it — it's not giving it back today.

So we're not getting cute. Same sharps, same discipline, right back to the well this afternoon.

The smart money's already loaded on three MLB spots — headlined by a plus-money dog the public won't touch.

We don't chase. We follow the money.

On the run with us, or waiting for us to slip?

alt_hooks: The hardest part of a +17-unit day isn't winning it. It's not giving it back the next day.
+17 units yesterday, and today everyone will tell you to book it and sit out. Winners don't flinch.

alternates.label: polarizing / defiance take
alternates.text:
+17 units yesterday — and today everyone will tell you to "book it and sit out."

That's how the public thinks. Winners don't flinch.

Same sharps, same plays, right back to the well this afternoon.

We don't protect a number. We press an edge.

Fading us today?

alternates.label: reply-before-reveal (cold binary)
alternates.text:
+17 units yesterday. Now the real test: not giving it back.

Before I post the side — the sharps are on a plus-money underdog in White Sox / Orioles. The public's on the favorite.

Which side do you want?

Reply before I reveal it.

options.standalone_rt_line: The hardest part of a +17-unit day isn't winning it. It's not giving it back the next day.

playbook_checks.the_blend_voice_mantra_close: We don't chase. We follow the money.
playbook_checks.forces_an_action: on the run or waiting for us to slip (replies)
